background.inspiredBy: Vitamin Well and HOLY both show how high-cleanliness backgrounds can keep functional information accessible and trustworthy.
background.name: Alpine White
background.role: default brand background — website sections, can whitespace, fact-led layouts

background.inspiredBy: Borrowed from the clean hydration world of Vitamin Well and LMNT, but softened so PEP feels refreshing rather than clinical.
background.name: Mist Blue
background.role: cool refreshment background — social cards, product callout panels, venue cards

background.inspiredBy: NOCCO’s performance credibility suggests a controlled dark mode can work, but PEP uses it sparingly and cleanly rather than aggressively.
background.name: Deep Training Navy
background.role: premium dark background — website hero overlays, night-mode story ads, reversed lockups

competitorInspiration: Across Tier 1 and Tier 2, the strongest lesson is that functional drinks win when the palette supports benefit clarity: HOLY, LMNT, NOCCO and Vitamin Well all use high-contrast information systems rather than decorative lifestyle colour. PEP should borrow that rational confidence while making it lighter and more refreshing than hardcore performance drinks. From Tier 3, brands like Poppi, OLIPOP, Recess, Seedlip and Lyre’s confirm what to avoid: cocktail-coded richness, vague wellness pastels, retro soda sweetness, and occasion-first styling that weakens the protein goal.

primary.inspiredBy: HOLY’s modern active-consumer clarity and LMNT’s direct functional punch support a strong blue as the core navigation and recognition colour.
primary.name: Kinetic Blue
primary.role: primary brand colour — wordmark, key borders, active UI states, lead benefit emphasis

primary.inspiredBy: NOCCO’s performance-adjacent credibility suggests a dark anchor, but this navy is cleaner and more premium than aggressive black supplement codes.
primary.name: Protein Navy
primary.role: structural anchor — large headlines, dark packaging panels, premium contrast

primary.inspiredBy: Vitamin Well and more fiz show that functional refreshment needs a fresh cue; mint gives lightness without becoming soda-like or wellness-vague.
primary.name: Fresh Mint
primary.role: refreshment accent — secondary highlights, flavour cues, motion lines, small illustrations

rejectPatterns: Black-red bodybuilding palettes, metallic flames, aggressive gradients, or extreme contrast associated with hardcore supplement RTDs.
Dusty wellness pastels used without functional contrast; these would make PEP feel like generic lifestyle soda rather than a protein tool.
Bar-cart colours such as amber, vermouth red, dark olive, smoked glass brown, or cocktail garnish greens that imply alcohol-first occasions.
Overly fruity juice palettes where orange, pink, and yellow dominate the system and make the drink feel childlike or novelty-led.

secondary.inspiredBy: LMNT’s punchy utility communication and NOCCO’s active energy cues support a sharp functional accent for key numbers and CTAs.
secondary.name: Citrus Pulse
secondary.role: high-visibility accent — CTA buttons, protein/calorie bursts, small energy markers

secondary.inspiredBy: Vitamin Well’s clean hydration language validates pale blue as a refreshment signal that keeps the system light.
secondary.name: Ice Blue
secondary.role: secondary panel colour — flavour backgrounds, ingredient/fact modules, calm supporting blocks

secondary.inspiredBy: HOLY’s simple hierarchy and Vitamin Well’s clean packaging language suggest precise separators rather than decorative clutter.
secondary.name: Cool Silver
secondary.role: utility neutral — rules, dividers, data ticks, can label structure

text.inspiredBy: HOLY and LMNT both rely on crisp readable text for benefit-first communication; near-black keeps PEP clear without medical severity.
text.name: Core Ink
text.role: primary text — headlines, body copy, product facts on light backgrounds

text.inspiredBy: Vitamin Well’s everyday functional feel supports softer secondary text for nutritional detail and explanatory copy.
text.name: Slate Grey
text.role: secondary text — captions, legal, supporting descriptions, inactive UI

text.inspiredBy: NOCCO-style dark performance environments require a clean reverse text colour, but PEP uses it with open spacing and calm structure.
text.name: Pure White
text.role: reverse text — dark hero sections, navy can panels, reversed badges
